**Major general** is a [military rank](/wiki/Military_rank "Military rank") used in many countries. It is derived from the older rank of [sergeant major general](/wiki/Sergeant_major_general "Sergeant major general").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In [English-speaking countries](/wiki/List_of_countries_by_English-speaking_population "List of countries by English-speaking population"), when appointed to a field command, a major general is typically in command of a [division](/wiki/Division_(military) "Division (military)") consisting of around 6,000 to 25,000 troops (several [regiments](/wiki/Regiment "Regiment") or [brigades](/wiki/Brigade "Brigade")). It is a rank that is subordinate to the rank of lieutenant general and senior to the rank of [brigadier](/wiki/Brigadier "Brigadier") or [brigadier general](/wiki/Brigadier_general "Brigadier general"). In the Commonwealth, major general is equivalent to the navy rank of [rear admiral](/wiki/Rear_admiral "Rear admiral"). In air forces with a separate rank structure (Commonwealth), major general is equivalent to [air vice-marshal](/wiki/Air_vice-marshal "Air vice-marshal").

In some countries including much of [Eastern Europe](/wiki/Eastern_Europe "Eastern Europe"), major general is the lowest of the [general officer](/wiki/General_officer "General officer") ranks without brigadier general rank.

In the sultanate of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"), the rank of Major general ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Mejar jeneral*) is used by the [Royal Brunei Land Force](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Land_Force "Royal Brunei Land Force") and the [Royal Brunei Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Air_Force "Royal Brunei Air Force"). #### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Canada")]

See also: [Canadian Armed Forces ranks and insignia](/wiki/Canadian_Armed_Forces_ranks_and_insignia "Canadian Armed Forces ranks and insignia")

In the [Canadian Armed Forces](/wiki/Canadian_Armed_Forces "Canadian Armed Forces"), the rank of major-general (MGen) ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *major-général*) is both a [Canadian Army](/wiki/Canadian_Army "Canadian Army") and [Royal Canadian Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Canadian_Air_Force "Royal Canadian Air Force") rank equivalent to the [Royal Canadian Navy](/wiki/Royal_Canadian_Navy "Royal Canadian Navy")'s rank of [rear-admiral](/wiki/Rear-admiral "Rear-admiral"). A major-general is a [general officer](/wiki/General_officer "General officer"), the equivalent of a naval [flag officer](/wiki/Flag_officer "Flag officer"). The major-general rank is senior to the ranks of [brigadier general](/wiki/Brigadier_general "Brigadier general") and [commodore](/wiki/Commodore_(rank) "Commodore (rank)"), and junior to [lieutenant-general](/wiki/Lieutenant-general_(Canada) "Lieutenant-general (Canada)") and [vice admiral](/wiki/Vice_admiral "Vice admiral"). Prior to 1968, the [Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Canadian_Air_Force "Royal Canadian Air Force") used the rank of [air vice-marshal](/wiki/Air_vice-marshal "Air vice-marshal"), instead.

The rank insignia for a major-general in the Royal Canadian Air Force is a wide braid under a single narrow braid on the cuff, as well as two silver maple leaves beneath a crossed sword and baton, all surmounted by St. Edward's Crown. In the Canadian Army, the rank insignia is a wide braid on the cuff, as well as two gold maple leaves beneath a crossed sword and baton, all surmounted by St. Edward's Crown. It is worn on the shoulder straps of the service dress tunic, and on slip-ons on other uniforms. On the visor of the service cap are two rows of gold oak leaves.

Major-generals are initially addressed as 'general' and name, as are all general officers; thereafter by subordinates as 'sir' or 'ma'am' as applicable in English ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *mon général*). Major-generals are normally entitled to [staff cars](/wiki/Staff_car "Staff car").

#### India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: India")]

Main article: [Major general (India)](/wiki/Major_general_(India) "Major general (India)")

#### Myanmar

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Myanmar")]

In Myanmar, a Major General rank is usually held by someone that is a Regional Military Command General Officer Commanding (Regional Commander or တိုင်းမှူး) or a Director such as Director of Defence Service Intelligence ([Khin Nyunt](/wiki/Khin_Nyunt "Khin Nyunt") for example)

#### New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: New Zealand")]

In the [New Zealand Army](/wiki/New_Zealand_Army "New Zealand Army"), major-general is the rank held by the chief of army (formerly the chief of general staff). The more senior rank of lieutenant-general is reserved for when an army officer holds the position of chief of defence force, who commands all of New Zealand's armed forces. This position is subject to rotation between the heads of the [New Zealand Air Force](/wiki/New_Zealand_Air_Force "New Zealand Air Force"), New Zealand Army, and [New Zealand Navy](/wiki/New_Zealand_Navy "New Zealand Navy").

#### Pakistan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Pakistan")]

Major general in the [Pakistan Army](/wiki/Pakistan_Army "Pakistan Army") is equivalent to rear admiral in the [Pakistan Navy](/wiki/Pakistan_Navy "Pakistan Navy") and air vice marshal in the [Pakistan Air Force](/wiki/Pakistan_Air_Force "Pakistan Air Force"). It is the lowest of the general officer ranks, ranking between brigadier and lieutenant general.

#### Portugal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Portugal")]

The rank of major-general was reintroduced in the [Portuguese Army](/wiki/Portuguese_Army "Portuguese Army"), [Portuguese Air Force](/wiki/Portuguese_Air_Force "Portuguese Air Force"), and [Portuguese National Republican Guard](/wiki/Portuguese_National_Republican_Guard "Portuguese National Republican Guard") in 1999, replacing the former rank of [brigadier](/wiki/Brigadier "Brigadier") in the role of brigade commander. As a rank, it had previously been used in the Army only for a brief period (from 1862 to 1864). It is equivalent to the rank of *contra-almirante* (rear-admiral) in the [Portuguese Navy](/wiki/Portuguese_Navy "Portuguese Navy"). In 2015, the rank of major-general was moved up one level, with the role of brigade commander being assumed by the below rank of brigadier-general.

In most of the 19th and first half of the 20th century, major-general was not used as a rank in the Portuguese military, but as an appointment title conferred to the general officer that acted as the military head of a [service branch](/wiki/Service_branch "Service branch"). The roles of Major-General of the Navy (*Major-General da Armada*) and Major-General of the Army (*Major-General do Exército*) became extinct in 1950, with their roles being unified in the then created [Chief of the General Staff of the Armed Forces](/wiki/General_Staff_of_the_Armed_Forces_(Portugal) "General Staff of the Armed Forces (Portugal)").

#### Russia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Russia")]

In the [Russian Army](/wiki/Russian_Army "Russian Army"), the rank 'major general' is known as [Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): генера́л-майо́р, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Russian "Romanization of Russian"): *generál-mayór*. It is equivalent to a British [brigadier](/wiki/Brigadier_(United_Kingdom) "Brigadier (United Kingdom)") or an American [brigadier general](/wiki/Brigadier_general_(United_States) "Brigadier general (United States)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Sri Lanka")]

Main article: [Major-general (Sri Lanka)](/wiki/Major-general_(Sri_Lanka) "Major-general (Sri Lanka)")

#### Turkey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Turkey")]

Main article: [tr:Tümgeneral](https://tr.wikipedia.org/wiki/T%C3%BCmgeneral "tr:Tümgeneral")

The Turkish Army and Air Force refer to the rank as *tümgeneral*. The Turkish Navy equivalent is *tümamiral*. The name is derived from *[tümen](/wiki/Tumen_(unit) "Tumen (unit)")*, the Turkish word for a military division (*tümen* itself is an older Turkish word meaning 10,000). Thus, linguistically, it is similar to the French equivalent for a major general, [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *général de division*.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: United States")]

Main article: [Major general (United States)](/wiki/Major_general_(United_States) "Major general (United States)")

In the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), the rank of major general exists in the [United States Air Force](/wiki/United_States_Air_Force "United States Air Force"), [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army"), [United States Marine Corps](/wiki/United_States_Marine_Corps "United States Marine Corps"), and [United States Space Force](/wiki/United_States_Space_Force "United States Space Force").

### *Generalmajor*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Major_general&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Generalmajor")]

Main articles: [Generalmajor](/wiki/Generalmajor "Generalmajor") and [Generalmajor (Sweden)](/wiki/Generalmajor_(Sweden) "Generalmajor (Sweden)")

***Generalmajor*** is the [Germanic](/wiki/Germanic_languages "Germanic languages") variant of major general, used in a number of [Central](/wiki/Central_Europe "Central Europe") and [Northern European](/wiki/Northern_Europe "Northern Europe") countries, including [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"), [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark"), [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), and [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden").
